title: Ensure any user that has not accessed WorkDocs in 30 days is set to inactive.

input_row.title: Ensure any user that has not accessed WorkDocs in 30 days is set to inactive.
input_row.description: User accounts that are not actively using the WorkDocs service should be set to inactive after a period of 30 days.
input_row.rationale: Inactive accounts may appear to not pose a problem but they can provide unauthorized access to files within WorkDocs.
input_row.audit: Perform the following steps to review list of users From the WorkDocs Admin control panel 1. Log in to WorkDocs as an Administrator 2. Under My Account, choose Open admin control panel. 3. Under Manage Users, choose Download user. 4. For Download user, choose All users 5. Review the file to determine if any users have not accessed WorkDocs in the past 30 days. If you find any users that have not accessed WorkDocs in the past 30 days refer to the remediation below.
input_row.remediation: Perform the steps below to disable a user's access by changing their status to Inactive. From the WorkDocs Admin control panel 1. Log in to WorkDocs as an Administrator. 2. Under My Account, click Open admin control panel. 3. Under Manage Users, choose the pencil icon next to the user's name that needs to be set as inactive. 4. Choose Inactive, and Click Save Changes The inactivated user no longer has access to your Amazon WorkDocs site.
input_row.impact: Changing a user to Inactive status does not delete their files, folders, or feedback from your Amazon WorkDocs site.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Log in to WorkDocs**:
   - Navigate to the WorkDocs Admin dashboard as an Administrator.
   - Access the admin control panel under "My Account".

2. **Download User List**:
   - Under "Manage Users", select the option to "Download user".
   - Choose "All users" to get a complete list of users in the organization.

3. **Review Last Access Dates**:
   - Open the downloaded user list file. Check the last access date column for each user.
   - Verify if any users have not accessed WorkDocs in the last 30 days.

4. **Identify Inactive Users**:
   - Note down the usernames of any users that have not accessed WorkDocs within the 30-day timeframe.

5. **Set Users to Inactive**:
   - For each identified inactive user, return to the WorkDocs Admin control panel.
   - Click on the pencil icon next to the user's name.
   - Change their status to "Inactive" and confirm by clicking "Save Changes".

6. **Expected Secure Configuration**:
    - Users who have not utilized the WorkDocs service in the last 30 days should be set to inactive.

7. **Confirm Compliance**:
   - After the changes are made, re-download the user list and ensure the status for the inactive users reflects the changes. This confirms that those users no longer have access to the WorkDocs site.
